10	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadMigrateCommand() *cobra.Command {
11		cmd := &cobra.Command{
12			Use:   "migrate",
13			Short: "Split active beads.jsonl into beads + beads-archive + attachments",
14			Long: `Split the existing .ddx/beads.jsonl into the modern layout:
15	
16	  * Closed beads' inline events are moved to per-bead sidecars under
17	    .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl (ADR-004).
18	  * Eligible closed beads are moved to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl using a
19	    permissive archival policy (TD-027).
20	
21	The command is idempotent — a second run with no further changes is a
22	no-op. All bead IDs remain addressable: 'ddx bead show', 'ddx bead list',
23	and 'ddx bead status' transparently consult the archive partner.`,
24			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
25			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
26				s := f.beadStore()
27				dryRun, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
28				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
29	
30				var (
31					ext  int
32					arch int
33				)
34				if dryRun {
35					st, err := s.MigrateDryRun()
36					if err != nil {
37						return err
38					}
39					ext, arch = st.EventsExternalized, st.Archived
40				} else {
41					st, err := s.Migrate()
42					if err != nil {
43						return err
44					}
45					ext, arch = st.EventsExternalized, st.Archived
46					if st.Changed() {
47						f.beadAutoCommit(fmt.Sprintf("migrate: externalize=%d archive=%d", ext, arch))
48					}
49				}
50				stats := migrateStatsView{EventsExternalized: ext, Archived: arch, DryRun: dryRun}
51	
52				if asJSON {
53					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
54					enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
55					return enc.Encode(stats)
56				}
57				out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
58				if dryRun {
59					fmt.Fprintln(out, "Dry run — no files were modified.")
60				}
61				fmt.Fprintf(out, "Externalized events: %d\n", ext)
62				fmt.Fprintf(out, "Archived beads:      %d\n", arch)
63				if ext == 0 && arch == 0 {
64					fmt.Fprintln(out, "No changes — already migrated.")
65				}
66				return nil
67			},
68		}
69		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output as JSON")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("dry-run", false, "Report what would change without writing")
71		return cmd
72	}
73	
74	type migrateStatsView struct {
75		EventsExternalized int  `json:"EventsExternalized"`
76		Archived           int  `json:"Archived"`
77		DryRun             bool `json:"DryRun,omitempty"`
78	}

15	// migrateSeed builds a closed bead with inline events and an updated
16	// timestamp far enough in the past that any future MinAge check would
17	// also accept it. Migrate uses MinAge=0 internally so the timestamp only
18	// matters for its archived_at stamp.
19	func migrateSeed(id, title string, withEvents bool) Bead {
20		t := time.Now().UTC().Add(-90 * 24 * time.Hour)
21		b := Bead{
22			ID:        id,
23			Title:     title,
24			Status:    StatusClosed,
25			Priority:  2,
26			IssueType: DefaultType,
27			CreatedAt: t.Add(-time.Hour),
28			UpdatedAt: t,
29		}
30		if withEvents {
31			b.Extra = map[string]any{
32				"events": []map[string]any{
33					{"kind": "review", "summary": "APPROVE", "body": "ok", "created_at": t.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)},
34					{"kind": "summary", "summary": "done", "body": "", "created_at": t.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)},
35				},
36				"closing_commit_sha": "deadbeef",
37			}
38		} else {
39			b.Extra = map[string]any{"closing_commit_sha": "deadbeef"}
40		}
41		return b
42	}

195	// TestMigrateLargeFixtureSplits seeds a synthetic >5MB beads.jsonl mostly
196	// composed of closed beads with inline events, runs Migrate, and asserts
197	// that the active file shrinks below 4MB while the archive partner picks
198	// up the closed rows and per-bead attachments are written.
199	func TestMigrateLargeFixtureSplits(t *testing.T) {
200		s := newTestStore(t)
201	
202		// Build ~600 closed beads each carrying a chunky inline event so the
203		// raw active file lands above the 4MB archive threshold.
204		old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-90 * 24 * time.Hour)
205		body := strings.Repeat("x", 8*1024) // 8KB body per event
206		beads := make([]Bead, 0, 605)
207		for i := 0; i < 600; i++ {
208			beads = append(beads, Bead{
209				ID:        fmt.Sprintf("ddx-l%04d", i),
210				Title:     "closed with bulky event",
211				Status:    StatusClosed,
212				Priority:  2,
213				IssueType: DefaultType,
214				CreatedAt: old.Add(-time.Hour),
215				UpdatedAt: old,
216				Extra: map[string]any{
217					"events": []map[string]any{
218						{"kind": "summary", "summary": "done", "body": body, "created_at": old.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)},
219					},
220					"closing_commit_sha": "deadbeef",
221				},
222			})
223		}
224		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
225			beads = append(beads, Bead{
226				ID:        fmt.Sprintf("ddx-o%d", i),
227				Title:     "open",
228				Status:    StatusOpen,
229				Priority:  2,
230				IssueType: DefaultType,
231				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour),
232				UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
233			})
234		}
235		require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll(beads))
236	
237		info, err := os.Stat(s.File)
238		require.NoError(t, err)
239		require.Greater(t, info.Size(), int64(4*1024*1024), "fixture must exceed 4MB to be a meaningful split test")
240	
241		stats, err := s.Migrate()
242		require.NoError(t, err)
243		assert.Equal(t, 600, stats.Archived)
244		assert.Equal(t, 600, stats.EventsExternalized)
245	
246		// AC2: active file < 4MB after migration.
247		info, err = os.Stat(s.File)
248		require.NoError(t, err)
249		assert.Less(t, info.Size(), int64(4*1024*1024), "active beads.jsonl must drop below 4MB after migration")
250	
251		// AC2: archive partner exists with the moved rows.
252		archInfo, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, BeadsArchiveCollection+".jsonl"))
253		require.NoError(t, err)
254		assert.Greater(t, archInfo.Size(), int64(0))
255	
256		// AC2: per-bead attachment sidecars exist for archived beads.
257		_, err = os.Stat(s.eventsAttachmentPath("ddx-l0000"))
258		require.NoError(t, err)
259	
260		// AC3 / AC6: re-running is a no-op.
261		second, err := s.Migrate()
262		require.NoError(t, err)
263		assert.False(t, second.Changed())
264	}

1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.

39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}

67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")

92		}

94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"path/filepath"
5		"sort"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// CollectionID is the logical name of a bead-backed collection.
10	// "beads" is the active queue; future collections (e.g. "beads-archive",
11	// "exec-runs", "agent-sessions") share the same on-disk format and store
12	// API but live under their own files and locks.
13	type CollectionID string
14	
15	// CollectionSpec is the shipping description of one collection. It maps a
16	// logical name to the concrete on-disk artifacts the JSONL backend uses.
17	//
18	// Per TD-027 §(a), the registry is an in-process table seeded at startup;
19	// it is not user-editable and DDx does not auto-discover collections from
20	// disk. Additional fields from TD-027 (QueueSemantics, ArchivePartner,
21	// Attachments) are deliberately omitted at this stage — TD-027 C3 will
22	// introduce them when archive logic lands. This bead (C2) is scoped to the
23	// storage-engine refactor: registry shape plus path/lock decoupling.
24	type CollectionSpec struct {
25		// ID is the logical collection name, e.g. "beads".
26		ID CollectionID
27		// JSONLFile is the file name (not a full path) under the .ddx
28		// directory that the JSONL backend reads and writes. Empty means
29		// "<id>.jsonl".
30		JSONLFile string
31		// LockDirName is the lock directory name under the .ddx directory.
32		// Empty means "<id>.lock".
33		LockDirName string
34	}
35	
36	// FileName returns the JSONL filename for this collection, falling back to
37	// the conventional "<id>.jsonl" form when JSONLFile is unset.
38	func (c CollectionSpec) FileName() string {
39		if c.JSONLFile != "" {
40			return c.JSONLFile
41		}
42		return string(c.ID) + ".jsonl"
43	}
44	
45	// LockName returns the lock-directory name for this collection, falling
46	// back to the conventional "<id>.lock" form when LockDirName is unset.
47	func (c CollectionSpec) LockName() string {
48		if c.LockDirName != "" {
49			return c.LockDirName
50		}
51		return string(c.ID) + ".lock"
52	}
53	
54	// PathsUnder returns the absolute file and lock paths for this collection
55	// rooted at the given .ddx directory.
56	func (c CollectionSpec) PathsUnder(ddxDir string) (file, lockDir string) {
57		return filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.FileName()), filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.LockName())
58	}
59	
60	// Registry is a table of known collection specs keyed by ID. Lookups for
61	// IDs that are not registered are not an error: NewStore synthesizes a
62	// conventional spec on the fly so existing call sites that use ad-hoc
63	// collection names (e.g. test fixtures) keep working. The registry is the
64	// authoritative source for the shipping collections that DDx itself
65	// instantiates.
66	type Registry struct {
67		mu    sync.RWMutex
68		specs map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec
69	}
70	
71	// NewRegistry returns an empty registry.
72	func NewRegistry() *Registry {
73		return &Registry{specs: make(map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec)}
74	}
75	
76	// Register adds or replaces a spec.
77	func (r *Registry) Register(spec CollectionSpec) {
78		r.mu.Lock()
79		defer r.mu.Unlock()
80		r.specs[spec.ID] = spec
81	}
82	
83	// Lookup returns the spec for id and whether it was registered. Callers
84	// that want a usable spec for any name should prefer Resolve.
85	func (r *Registry) Lookup(id CollectionID) (CollectionSpec, bool) {
86		r.mu.RLock()
87		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
88		spec, ok := r.specs[id]
89		return spec, ok
90	}
91	
92	// Resolve returns the spec for id, synthesizing a conventional one (with
93	// "<id>.jsonl" / "<id>.lock") when id is not registered. This keeps the
94	// JSONL backend's per-collection layout uniform whether or not a name is
95	// in the shipping table.
96	func (r *Registry) Resolve(id CollectionID) CollectionSpec {
97		if spec, ok := r.Lookup(id); ok {
98			return spec
99		}
100		return CollectionSpec{ID: id}
101	}
102	
103	// IDs returns the registered collection IDs in lexical order.
104	func (r *Registry) IDs() []CollectionID {
105		r.mu.RLock()
106		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
107		out := make([]CollectionID, 0, len(r.specs))
108		for id := range r.specs {
109			out = append(out, id)
110		}
111		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i] < out[j] })
112		return out
113	}
114	
115	// defaultRegistry is the process-wide registry. It ships with the active
116	// "beads" collection and its archive partner "beads-archive" (TD-027 §a).
117	// Additional collections (exec-runs, agent-sessions) will be registered by
118	// their owning beads.
119	var defaultRegistry = func() *Registry {
120		r := NewRegistry()
121		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
122			ID:          DefaultCollection,
123			JSONLFile:   DefaultCollection + ".jsonl",
124			LockDirName: DefaultCollection + ".lock",
125		})
126		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
127			ID:          BeadsArchiveCollection,
128			JSONLFile:   BeadsArchiveCollection + ".jsonl",
129			LockDirName: BeadsArchiveCollection + ".lock",
130		})
131		return r
132	}()
133	
134	// DefaultRegistry returns the process-wide collection registry.
135	func DefaultRegistry() *Registry {
136		return defaultRegistry
137	}

// TestMigrateCommandToAxon covers AC §1 (the --to axon flag exists and is
// documented), AC §3 (idempotent across runs), and the source-files-intact
// contract from the bead description.
func TestMigrateCommandToAxon(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)

	helpOut, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--help")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, helpOut, "--to", "--help must advertise the --to flag")
	assert.Contains(t, helpOut, "axon", "--help must advertise the axon target")

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-90 * 24 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339)
	activeRows := strings.Join([]string{
		`{"id":"ddx-mta-1","title":"open","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `"}`,
		`{"id":"ddx-mta-2","title":"in_progress","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alice","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","events":[{"kind":"claimed","summary":"alice claimed","actor":"alice","created_at":"` + old + `"}]}`,
	}, "\n") + "\n"
	archiveRows := strings.Join([]string{
		`{"id":"ddx-mta-3","title":"closed and archived","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"` + old + `","updated_at":"` + old + `","closing_commit_sha":"abc1230000","archived_at":"` + old + `"}`,
	}, "\n") + "\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl"), []byte(activeRows), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads-archive.jsonl"), []byte(archiveRows), 0o644))

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--to", "axon", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var view struct {
		BeadsMigrated  int    `json:"BeadsMigrated"`
		EventsMigrated int    `json:"EventsMigrated"`
		To             string `json:"To"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &view))
	assert.Equal(t, 3, view.BeadsMigrated)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, view.EventsMigrated)
	assert.Equal(t, "axon", view.To)

	// Source files left intact (operator removes after verification).
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads.jsonl"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "beads-archive.jsonl"))

	// Axon collection files written.
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_beads.jsonl"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_bead_events.jsonl"))

	// AC §3: a second invocation reports the same counts and produces
	// byte-identical files (no duplicates).
	beadsBefore, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_beads.jsonl"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	eventsBefore, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_bead_events.jsonl"))
	require.NoError(t, err)

	out2, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "migrate", "--to", "axon", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var view2 struct {
		BeadsMigrated  int `json:"BeadsMigrated"`
		EventsMigrated int `json:"EventsMigrated"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out2), &view2))
	assert.Equal(t, 3, view2.BeadsMigrated)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, view2.EventsMigrated)

	beadsAfter, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_beads.jsonl"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	eventsAfter, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "axon", "ddx_bead_events.jsonl"))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, string(beadsBefore), string(beadsAfter))
	assert.Equal(t, string(eventsBefore), string(eventsAfter))
}

feat(bead): add migrate --to axon for JSONL → axon backend [ddx-cb63cdfc]

Adds a one-shot migration command that reads .ddx/beads.jsonl and
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl and writes them losslessly into the axon
backend at .ddx/axon/. Source files are left intact so the operator
can verify with `ddx bead export | diff` before removing them.

The migration always reads through fresh JSONL backends regardless of
the configured store backend so it is deterministic. Active wins on
duplicate IDs, matching TD-027 §(b) precedence. Re-running on the same
source state produces a byte-identical axon snapshot (AxonBackend.WriteAll
overwrites both collection files in temp+rename style), satisfying the
idempotency contract.

  fixture (3 active + 2 archived beads, 4 inline events) and asserts the
  axon-backed export matches the JSONL export bead-for-bead.
- TestMigrateToAxonNoSources: empty .ddx is a no-op.
- TestMigrateCommandToAxon: covers --help advertising the flag and the
  CLI-level idempotency contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Implemented `ddx bead migrate --to axon` (commit `4024c5c2`):

**AC coverage:**
1. **Command exists & documented** — `--to` flag wired in `cmd/bead_migrate.go`, advertised in `--help` (asserted in `TestMigrateCommandToAxon`).
2. **Round-trip lossless** — `TestMigrateToAxonRoundTripFromFixture` reads fixture into JSONL store, captures pre-migration export, runs `MigrateToAxon`, then exports from a fresh axon-backed store and asserts bead-for-bead equality.
3. **Idempotent** — second invocation reports identical counts and produces byte-identical `ddx_beads.jsonl`/`ddx_bead_events.jsonl` (asserted in both unit and cmd tests).
4. **Test fixture** — `cli/internal/bead/testdata/migrate_axon_{active,archive}.jsonl` covers open/in-progress/closed beads, inline events, and an archived bead with no events.

**Source files preserved** — explicit assertion that `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` survive the migration (operator removes after verification).
